en: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, To the church of God that is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia:
zh: 奉 神旨意作基督耶穌使徒的保羅，和提摩太弟兄，寫信給在哥林多 神的教會，和全亞該亞所有的聖徒。

en: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
zh: 願恩惠平安從我們的父 神和主耶穌基督臨到你們。

en: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
zh: 我們主耶穌基督的父 神是應當稱頌的。他是滿有憐憫的父，賜各樣安慰的 神。

en: who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
zh: 我們在一切患難中， 神都安慰我們，使我們能用他所賜的安慰，去安慰那些在各樣患難中的人。

en: For as we share abundantly in Christ's sufferings, so through Christ we share abundantly in comfort too.
zh: 我們既然多受基督所受的痛苦，就靠著基督多得安慰。

en: If we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; and if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which you experience when you patiently endure the same sufferings that we suffer.
zh: 如果我們遭遇患難，那是要使你們得著安慰，得著拯救；如果我們得到安慰，也是要使你們得到安慰。這安慰使你們能夠忍受我們所受那樣的痛苦。

en: Our hope for you is unshaken, for we know that as you share in our sufferings, you will also share in our comfort.
zh: 我們對你們的盼望是堅定的，因為知道你們既然一同受痛苦，也必照樣同得安慰。

en: For we do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, of the affliction we experienced in Asia. For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself.
zh: 弟兄們，我們在亞西亞遭受的患難，我們很想讓你們知道。那時我們受到了過於我們所能忍受的壓力，甚至活下去的希望都沒有了，

en: Indeed, we felt that we had received the sentence of death. But that was to make us rely not on ourselves but on God who raises the dead.
zh: 而且斷定自己是必死的了；然而，這正是要我們不倚靠自己，只倚靠那叫死人復活的 神。

en: He delivered us from such a deadly peril, and he will deliver us. On him we have set our hope that he will deliver us again.
zh: 他救我們脫離了那極大的死亡，而且他還要救我們，我們希望他將來仍要救我們。

en: You also must help us by prayer, so that many will give thanks on our behalf for the blessing granted us through the prayers of many.
zh: 請你們一同用禱告支持我們，好使許多人為著我們所蒙的恩獻上感謝。這恩是藉著許多人的代求而得到的。

en: For our boast is this: the testimony of our conscience that we behaved in the world with simplicity and godly sincerity, not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God, and supremely so toward you.
zh: 我們引以為榮的，就是我們處世為人，是本著 神的聖潔和真誠，不是靠著人的聰明，而是靠著 神的恩典，對你們更是這樣，這是我們的良心可以作證的。

en: For we are not writing to you anything other than what you read and acknowledge and I hope you will fully acknowledge-
zh: 我們現在寫給你們的，不外是你們可以宣讀、可以明白的。

en: just as you did partially acknowledge us, that on the day of our Lord Jesus you will boast of us as we will boast of you.
zh: 正如你們對我們已經有些認識，我盼望你們可以徹底明白：在我們的主耶穌的日子，我們是你們的光榮，你們也是我們的光榮。

en: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to come to you first, so that you might have a second experience of grace.
zh: 我既然有這樣的信念，就打算先到你們那裡，使你們再一次得到恩惠，

en: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and to come back to you from Macedonia and have you send me on my way to Judea.
zh: 然後經過你們那裡，往馬其頓去，再從馬其頓回到你們中間，讓你們給我送行往猶太去。

en: Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this? Do I make my plans according to the flesh, ready to say "Yes, yes" and "No, no" at the same time?
zh: 我這樣決定，難道是反覆不定嗎？我所決定的，難道是體貼肉體而定，使我忽是忽非嗎？

en: As surely as God is faithful, our word to you has not been Yes and No.
zh: 神是信實的，我們向你們所傳的道並不是“是”而又“非”的，

en: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, whom we proclaimed among you, Silvanus and Timothy and I, was not Yes and No, but in him it is always Yes.
zh: 因為我、西拉和提摩太，在你們中間所宣揚的耶穌基督， 神的兒子，並不是“是”而又“非”的，在他總是“是”的。

en: For all the promises of God find their Yes in him. That is why it is through him that we utter our Amen to God for his glory.
zh: 因為 神的一切應許，在基督裡都是“是”的，為此我們藉著他說“阿們”，使榮耀歸於 神。

en: And it is God who establishes us with you in Christ, and has anointed us,
zh: 那在基督裡堅定我們和你們，又膏抹我們的，就是 神。

en: and who has also put his seal on us and given us his Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee.
zh: 他在我們身上蓋了印，就是賜聖靈在我們心裡作憑據。

en: But I call God to witness against me- it was to spare you that I refrained from coming again to Corinth.
zh: 我呼求 神給我作證，我沒有再到哥林多來，是要寬容你們。

en: Not that we lord it over your faith, but we work with you for your joy, for you stand firm in your faith.
zh: 我們並不是要轄制你們的信仰，而是要作你們的同工，使你們喜樂，因為你們在信仰上已經站穩了。
